statement: The average time between EU adoption of a legal act and its incorporation into the EEA Agreement via Joint Committee Decision has been approximately 12–18 months, though backlogs have at times exceeded 500 outstanding acts. As of 2024, the EFTA Surveillance Authority reported an incorporation backlog of around 400–500 acts awaiting Joint Committee decisions.
caveats: The backlog fluctuates and has been a persistent concern. Some delays are due to constitutional requirements in EFTA states (particularly Norway and Iceland requiring parliamentary approval for certain acts). Critics argue the lag creates legal uncertainty; defenders note it allows adaptation time.

related_entries.statement: As of late 2025, approximately 13,000 EU legal acts (directives and regulations) had been incorporated into the EEA Agreement and transposed into Icelandic law since the Agreement entered into force in 1994. This represents the cumulative acquis adopted by Iceland through the EEA Joint Committee decision process.

The EFTA Surveillance Authority (ESA) monitors compliance of EEA EFTA states with EEA law, mirroring the European Commission's enforcement role. ESA can issue reasoned opinions, bring infringement cases before the EFTA Court, and enforce state aid rules including ordering recovery of unlawful aid. Between 2012 and 2024, ESA opened an average of 30–50 new infringement cases per year against Iceland, Norway, and Liechtenstein combined.
